name: nav-marketing
role: Top-level scope for the marketing-site header. Polymorphic <header role='banner'> containing optional announcement bar, brand block, primary nav list, and CTA cluster. Four variants — default (solid surface), sticky (position: sticky with condensed-on-scroll behaviour), transparent (renders over hero artwork until scrolled), compact (smaller height for sub-pages and in-app shell).

semanticStructure: <header role='banner'> — the page-banner landmark; only one per page
(optional) <div data-pattern='nav-announcement-bar'> — promotional or status banner above the main nav
<nav-primary-list> — middle <ul> of nav-items (some may host dropdowns)
<nav-cta-cluster> — right-anchored buttons (Log in / Sign up etc.) composing pattern-button-system
<nav-mobile-toggle> — hamburger button, visible at narrow widths only

variants.default: Solid --surface ground at all scroll positions. No special scroll handling. Used on docs landings, blog posts, secondary marketing.
variants.sticky: position: sticky; top: 0. At scroll > 80px the nav transitions to a condensed height with --surface-elevated background, --shadow-md, and tighter padding. Composed via data-state='scrolled' attribute set by host JS.
variants.transparent: Renders over the hero artwork (no background, no shadow) until scroll-threshold is crossed. Then promotes to sticky+scrolled visual treatment. Used on marketing home with hero imagery.
variants.compact: Smaller padding-y and font-size. Used on sub-pages, in-app marketing surfaces, embedded contexts.

scrollBehaviour.transitionFromTransparent: On scroll past threshold: background fade --surface→--surface-elevated; shadow fade none→--shadow-md; padding-y compress --space-4→--space-3; transition: --transition-default.
scrollBehaviour.respectsReducedMotion: If prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, the scrolled-state transition collapses to instant (no fade).

ariaPatterns.bannerLandmark: <header role='banner'> is the canonical Page Banner landmark. Only one per page.
ariaPatterns.navLandmark: <nav aria-label='Primary'> distinguishes from footer nav and from in-page sub-nav. The label MUST be 'Primary' (or localised equivalent) — keep it stable.
ariaPatterns.skipLink:
Add Skip to main content as the first focusable element. Visually hidden until focused; jumps focus to the page's
landmark.
ariaPatterns.stickyAnnouncement: Sticky nav should NOT trap focus or break tab order. The skip-link bypasses it for keyboard users on long pages.

name: nav-brand
role: Brand identity block in the left side of the nav. Renders as <a> linking to '/' with SVG wordmark or logo-mark inside + optional adjacent product-name text. The link's aria-label names the destination explicitly so screen readers announce 'Galley home, link' rather than the SVG name.

semanticStructure: <svg aria-hidden='true'> — the wordmark / logo-mark SVG (aria-hidden because the aria-label on the <a> already names the destination)
(optional) <span class='product-name'> — explicit product-name text adjacent to the mark

ariaPatterns.ariaLabelNaming: The <a aria-label> MUST name the destination ('Galley home', 'Quoin home', etc.). SVG inside is aria-hidden because the link's own label already announces the destination.
ariaPatterns.wordmarkVsLogomark: Wordmark contains the typeset brand name; logo-mark is the abstract icon. Either can be used in nav-brand; both can stack. If both are present, mark first then wordmark text.

name: nav-primary-list
role: Middle <ul> of primary nav items. Each <li> contains either a <nav-item> link or a <nav-item> dropdown-trigger (which opens a <nav-dropdown-panel>). Renders inline at desktop; collapses into the mobile drawer at narrow widths.

meta.mobileBehaviour: At max-width: 960px, the entire <nav-primary-list> hides; the mobile toggle reveals nav-mobile-overlay which contains the same list rendered vertically.

name: nav-item
role: Single nav anchor or dropdown trigger. Three variants — link (plain anchor to a route), dropdown (button that toggles a nav-dropdown-panel; sets aria-expanded), current (the active route; carries aria-current='page' + underline). All 8 microstates supported.

variants.link: <a href='/route'> — plain anchor. Renders with hover background and accent color on hover.
variants.dropdown: <button aria-expanded='false' aria-controls='<panel-id>' aria-haspopup='true'> — toggles the nav-dropdown-panel. Trailing chevron rotates 180° when expanded. Click + Enter + Space all activate; Escape closes when focus is inside panel.
variants.current: <a href='/current' aria-current='page'> — same as link but with --text-emphasis colour, --font-weight-semibold, and a 2px underline indicator in --accent at the bottom of the item.

ariaPatterns.dropdownTrigger: role=button + aria-haspopup='true' + aria-expanded='true|false' + aria-controls='<panel-id>'. Panel is positioned absolutely relative to the trigger. Click outside panel or Escape closes it.
ariaPatterns.currentPageMarker: aria-current='page' on the active route's <a>. Visible underline + colour shift.
ariaPatterns.keyboardModel: Tab through nav-items in source order. On a dropdown trigger, Enter/Space opens panel + moves focus to first focusable inside. Inside the panel, Tab cycles through items; Shift+Tab exits backwards; Escape closes + returns focus to trigger.

name: nav-dropdown-panel
role: Submenu panel anchored to a nav-item dropdown trigger. Two registers — simple (a vertical list of links) and mega (multi-column panel with featured cells, supports composing feature-cell from pattern-feature-grid in P1). Renders as an absolutely-positioned <div> with role='menu' OR a regular <ul> with implicit menu semantics. State is controlled by the trigger's aria-expanded.

registers.simple: Vertical list of links. Each link can have a short description below it. Width matches trigger or wider; max ~320px.
registers.mega: Multi-column panel; each column can have a heading + link list, OR can host a <feature-cell> (composing pattern-feature-grid in P1 for visual feature highlights). Width spans 600-1200px depending on column count.

meta.positioning: Anchored to trigger button with CSS anchor positioning where supported; falls back to JS-positioned absolute layout. Default to bottom-start aligned. On viewport edge collision, flip to bottom-end.

ariaPatterns.panelRole: <div role='menu'> if treating as a pure menu (keyboard nav with arrow keys); <ul> with no role for navigation-style panels (Tab traversal). Default: ul (lower friction for users).
ariaPatterns.ariaLabelledby: <div aria-labelledby='<trigger-id>'> wires the panel to its trigger so screen readers announce the section name.
ariaPatterns.escapeClose: Escape closes the panel + returns focus to the trigger.
ariaPatterns.clickOutside: Click outside the panel closes it. Implement via document-level click handler that checks event.target.

name: nav-cta-cluster
role: Right-anchored group of action buttons. Composes <action-button> from pattern-button-system. Typical cluster: a 'Log in' link button + a 'Sign up' / 'Get started' / 'Start free trial' primary button. May include a status indicator (e.g. github-stars badge) or theme-switcher.

registers.link-plus-cta: Ghost-intent 'Log in' link + primary-intent 'Sign up' CTA. The canonical marketing pattern.
registers.cta-only: Single primary CTA. Used when there's only one conversion path (e.g. 'Get started' for products with no login).
registers.dual-cta: Two CTAs with distinct intents — e.g. 'Pricing' (secondary) + 'Sign up' (primary).
registers.extended: Ghost link + secondary + primary. Used on flagship marketing home where 'Talk to sales' / 'View pricing' / 'Start free trial' coexist.

ariaPatterns.groupLabel: <div role='group' aria-label='Account actions'> on the cluster wrapper to give the group context.
ariaPatterns.primaryCTA: The rightmost button should be the highest-intent action (intent=primary). Lefter buttons should de-escalate intent.

name: nav-mobile-toggle
role: Hamburger / close button shown at narrow widths only. Toggles the nav-mobile-overlay. Maintains aria-expanded + aria-controls pointing to the overlay's id. Composes <action-button intent='ghost' size='md'> from pattern-button-system with icon-only content.

meta.iconBehaviour: Renders ☰ (hamburger) when closed, ✕ when open. Both icons are aria-hidden because the button's aria-label communicates state explicitly.
meta.visibility: Hidden via display: none at min-width: 961px. Visible at max-width: 960px.

ariaPatterns.expandedState: aria-expanded='true|false' on the button; aria-controls points to the overlay's id.
ariaPatterns.ariaLabelStatePair: aria-label='Open menu' when closed; aria-label='Close menu' when open. Update both the icon and the label on toggle.
ariaPatterns.focusReturn: On close, return focus to where it was before the overlay opened. If a link inside the overlay was activated, focus returns to body's focused target (the new page's first focusable).
ariaPatterns.focusTrap: When overlay is open, focus is trapped inside it. Tab cycles through overlay items; Tab from last item returns to first; Shift+Tab from first goes to last.

name: nav-mobile-overlay
role: Full-screen drawer revealed by nav-mobile-toggle at narrow widths. Hosts the same primary nav links + CTA cluster as desktop, plus the brand wordmark at the top. Backdrop dim + body scroll-lock when open. Slides in from the right by default; from the top is a secondary option.

registers.right-slide: Slides in from the right edge. Takes 320-400px of viewport width. Backdrop dims the rest.
registers.top-sheet: Slides down from the top. Takes full width × ~75% of viewport height. Backdrop dims the bottom.

meta.bodyScrollLock: When open, set overflow: hidden on <body> to prevent background scrolling. Restore on close.

ariaPatterns.dialogRole: role='dialog' + aria-modal='true' makes the overlay a modal dialog. Focus is trapped inside until closed.
ariaPatterns.focusTrap: On open: focus moves to the first focusable inside the overlay (typically the close button). Tab cycles through overlay items; Shift+Tab cycles backwards.
ariaPatterns.escapeClose: Escape key closes the overlay + returns focus to the toggle button.
ariaPatterns.scrimDismiss: Clicking the backdrop closes the overlay (same as Escape).

name: nav-announcement-bar
role: Optional banner row above the main nav. Hosts a short promotional / status message + optional CTA link + optional dismiss button. Renders in an accent-tinted strip with sufficient contrast. Often used for product launches, status incidents, or seasonal promotions.

tones.info: Neutral accent-recede background. Used for informational messages (e.g. 'New: Quoin v0.2 ships pattern packs').
tones.promo: Full --accent background. Used for promotional messages (e.g. 'Launch week — 20% off Pro through Friday').
tones.warning: --warning background. Used for non-critical service degradations (e.g. 'Higher than usual latency in EU region').
tones.critical: --critical background. Used for service-affecting incidents (e.g. 'API outage — see status.galley.harrow.haus').

ariaPatterns.regionLabel: <div role='region' aria-label='Announcement'> distinguishes from main page content.
ariaPatterns.liveOnUpdate: If the banner content updates dynamically (e.g. status changes), add aria-live='polite' so screen readers announce the change.
ariaPatterns.dismissPersistence: On dismiss, persist the state to localStorage so the banner stays dismissed for the user. Re-show on banner ID change.
